name: HMS Leyland (FY 103)
events: 12 Oct 1939 HMS H 44 (Lt. H.A.V. Haggard, RN) conducted exercises off Portland with HMS Leyland (Lt.Cdr. A. Wilkinson, RNR). (1)

27 Mar 1941 HrMs O 21 (Lt.Cdr. J.F. van Dulm, RNN) carried out A/S exercises off Gibraltar with HMS Alouette (Lt. R.F.R. Yarde-Buller, RNVR), HMS Erin (A/Cdr. J.O. Davies, RNR), HMS Lord Hotham (Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR) and HMS Leyland (Skr. H. Crighton, RNR). (2)
23 Nov 1941 HrMs O 9 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HMCS Rosthern (Cdr. P.B. Cross, RCNVR) and HMS Leyland (Lt. G.M. Dixon, RANVR). (3)
25 Nov 1941 HrMs O 9 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HMS Leyland (Lt. G.M. Dixon, RANVR) and HMS Newark (Lt.Cdr. R.H.W. Atkins, RN). (3)
11 May 1942 At 1600 B/11, HMS P 44 (Lt. T.E. Barlow, RN) arrived at Gibraltar.
She was escorted in by HMS Leyland (T/Lt. A.K. Nears, RNR) which had joined around 1150 B/11. (4)
13 Aug 1942 Around 1700 A/13, the damaged HMS Talisman (Lt.Cdr. M. Willmott, DSO, RN) arrived at Gibraltar. She had been escorted in by HMS Leyland (T/Lt. A.K. Nears, RNR). (5)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
